message_header.subject: Request for Temporary Access to 4th Floor Storage Room and Clarification on New Keycard Policy

message_body.message:
Dear Jordan,

I am writing to request temporary access to the 4th floor storage room (Room 4B-Storage) from Monday, 2025-12-15 through Friday, 2025-12-19, and to clarify a few details regarding the new keycard access policy that went into effect this quarter.

Background and purpose of request:
I manage logistics and physical materials for the Marketing Department’s client events and internal town halls. We are currently preparing for the Q1 Client Innovation Showcase scheduled for 2026-01-09 in the main auditorium. As part of the preparation, my team needs to inventory, clean, and re-label several large display units, retractable banners, portable podiums, and branded demo kiosks that are stored in the 4th floor storage room.

Historically, I have had an individually issued physical key (Key #4B-17) that allowed me access to this room. However, per the email circulated by Facilities on 2025-11-01, all physical keys for common storage areas were to be turned in and replaced by keycard-only access. I turned in my key to the Facilities front desk on 2025-11-05. Since then, I have not been able to access the storage room and have been coordinating with your team to have someone let me in as needed. This has become increasingly difficult as we approach year-end and everyone’s schedules are more constrained.

Specific access requested:
1. Dates requested: 2025-12-15 (Monday) through 2025-12-19 (Friday)
2. Times requested: 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM each day (with flexibility if you prefer a narrower window)
3. Location: 4th Floor Storage Room, labeled "4B-Storage" on the floor plan, across from Conference Room 4B-3
4. Purpose:
   - Conduct a full inventory of all Marketing-owned materials currently stored in 4B-Storage
   - Identify and tag outdated or damaged items for disposal, donation, or recycling
   - Clean and re-pack all items that will be used for the 2026-01-09 Client Innovation Showcase
   - Photograph and document the layout of the storage room to create a simple visual reference guide for new team members
5. Type of access requested:
   - Ideally, temporary keycard access linked to my existing employee badge (ID: 47219)
   - If temporary keycard access is not possible, scheduled access windows where a Facilities team member can unlock the door for us (preferably at 9:00 AM and 2:00 PM each day)

Team members involved:
I will be working with two colleagues from Marketing who will assist with the inventory and cleanup:
- Name: Daniel Martinez (Event Specialist, Marketing) – Employee ID: 48307
- Name: Priya Desai (Marketing Coordinator) – Employee ID: 49112

If possible, I would appreciate it if you could authorize temporary keycard access for all three of us for the dates and times listed above. If Facilities policy allows only a single point of contact, I am comfortable being the sole person granted access and will be responsible for supervising Daniel and Priya while we are in the storage room.

Questions about the new keycard policy:
In addition to the access request, I want to make sure I fully understand the new keycard policy so that I can plan more effectively for future events and avoid last-minute issues:

1. Duration of temporary access:
   - When temporary access is granted for project-based work (like pre-event setup), is there a standard maximum duration (e.g., one week, one month), or is it determined case-by-case?

2. After-hours access:
   - On rare occasions, we need to access storage after regular business hours (for example, to load materials into a rental van the evening before an offsite event). Is it possible to request after-hours access on a one-off basis, and if so, what lead time do you typically need to process that request?

3. Shared access for cross-functional teams:
   - Our marketing storage space is occasionally used by Sales and Product teams when they borrow demo units or signage for client meetings. Under the new policy, should we centralize all access requests through Marketing (so that I remain the single point of contact), or can cross-functional partners request access directly through Facilities if they are working on approved company events?

4. Safety and compliance expectations:
   - I understand from the policy memo that storage rooms must remain compliant with fire codes and that items may not block sprinklers, emergency exits, or electrical panels. We will be reorganizing some shelving units and stacking items differently to improve accessibility. Are there any updated guidelines, diagrams, or maximum height limits for stacked boxes that we should follow so we don’t inadvertently create a safety issue while trying to tidy up?

Commitments and assurances:
To help you feel comfortable granting this temporary access, I want to reiterate the following:
- We will keep a detailed list of all items removed from and returned to the storage room during this period.
- We will not store any personal items or non-work-related materials in the room.
- We will ensure that the room is left in a cleaner and more organized state than we found it, with clear walkways and no items blocking doors or panels.
- We will immediately report any damage, maintenance issues (e.g., flickering lights, water leaks, loose shelving), or safety concerns we notice while working in the space.

Contact information:
If you need to reach me to discuss this request or to clarify any of the details above, my contact information is below:
- Mobile: (312) 555-9472
- Office: (312) 555-6120
- Email: emily.thompson@northbridgecreative.com
- Office location: NorthBridge Creative, 225 W. Huron St., Suite 900, Chicago, IL 60654

I am happy to adjust the dates and times requested if they conflict with planned maintenance, inspections, or other scheduled use of the storage area. If it would be easier, I can also stop by your office in person this week to walk through the request and confirm what works best from your perspective.

Thank you in advance for your help with this. Having predictable access to the storage room next week will make a significant difference in our ability to prepare properly for the January client event and will also help us bring the space into better long-term order.

Sincerely,

Emily R. Thompson
Senior Project Coordinator, Marketing Department
NorthBridge Creative
